items.summary: Jaxon Smith-Njigba has reached agreement with the Seahawks on a four-year, $168.6 million extension that makes him the highest-paid wide receiver in NFL history, sources told ESPN.

items.summary: The Buccaneers are signing free agent linebacker Christian Rozeboom, agents Chris Gittings and Jeremiah Sirles told NFL Network on Monday.

items.title: HOF to honor 3 former NFL assistant coaches
items.summary: Influential special teams coach Mike Westhoff, former 49ers offensive line coach Bobb McKittrick and longtime defensive coach Ted Cottrell will be honored with the Pro Football Hall of Fame's Awards of Excellence for the 2026 class.

Other moves included signings to bolster the defensive line and secondary plus additional veteran re-signings. Colts.com [...]

items.title: Colts add 2 more former Lou Anarumo players: Cam Taylor-Britt and Akeem Davis-Gaither
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – It’s never wrong to connect the dots and consider relationships when teams look to add new talent and players seek to extend their careers. items.title: Colts former longtime WR great T.Y. Hilton officially announces retirement
items.summary: Former Indianapolis Colts wideout great T.Y. Hilton took to Twitter (or X?) to officially announce his retirement, having not played professional football since 2022. Now 36-years-old, the 11-year veteran formally finishes his highly productive pro career with 638 receptions for 9,812 total receiving yards (15.4 ypr. avg.) and 53 touchdown receptions during 146 career games […]

items.title: Former Colts WR T.Y. Hilton announces retirement from NFL
items.summary: Hilton, who spent a decade in Indianapolis, ranks third in Colts history in receptions (631) and receiving yards (9,691).

items.title: Colts sign free agent WR Nick Westbrook-Ikhine
items.summary: Westbrook-Ikhine played at Indiana University and has spent six seasons in the NFL with the Tennessee Titans (2024-24) and Miami Dolphins (2025).

items.title: Daniel Jones progressing well through Achilles injury, expects to be '100 percent' for Week 1 of 2026 season
items.summary: Jones, who officially signed a new contract with the Colts on Thursday, said he intends to be ready to play by Week 1 of the 2026 season, if not sooner.

items.title: Colts re-sign QB Daniel Jones
items.summary: Jones started 13 games for the Colts in 2025 and finished the season third in the NFL in yards per attempt (8.1), fourth in completion percentage (68 percent) and sixth in passer rating (100.2).

items.title: Alec Pierce: This is where I wanted to be
